build.//buildCommand: Two concerns: (1) `npm install` not `ci` because Railpack mounts /app/node_modules/.cache and ci's wipe EBUSYs the mount. (2) --include=dev because Railway sets NODE_ENV=production at the service level, which makes `npm install` skip devDependencies — without it autoprefixer/tailwindcss/typescript/postcss aren't on disk and next/font fails with 'Cannot find module autoprefixer'.
build.//: watchPatterns are evaluated relative to the repo root. Limiting to HOA-ENTERPRISE/** stops sibling-app changes from triggering a redeploy of this service.

deploy.//: Next.js standalone server. `output: 'standalone'` in next.config.js makes .next/standalone/server.js self-contained — Railway just runs it. PORT is injected by Railway and Next.js reads it automatically. NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 is required for sleepApplication to work: Next.js phones home telemetry, which counts as outbound traffic and would keep the service perpetually awake, defeating App Sleeping.
deploy.//sleepApplication: App Sleeping: sleep after ~10 min with no outbound traffic; the next inbound request wakes it (first request may 502 during cold start). This service is low-traffic, so idle RAM — the dominant cost — drops toward zero. Health checks are inbound and do not keep it awake.
